popularStory.headline: Ferdinand II de León
popularStory.description: Ferdinand II de León ou Fernando II, né en 1137, mort à Benavente le 22 janvier 1188, fut roi de Léon et de Galice de 1157 à 1188. Il était fils d'Alphonse VII, roi de Castille, Léon et Galice, et de …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ferdinand II De León於1137年8月15日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安薩莫拉貝納文特時，他的父親Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León32歲，母親Berenguela de Barcelona21歲。他於1165年6月與Urraca Of Portugal結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1188年1月22日在他的家鄉享年50歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ferdinand II De León于1137年8月15日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安薩莫拉貝納文特时，他的父亲Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León32岁，母亲Berenguela de Barcelona21岁。他于1165年6月与Urraca Of Portugal结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1188年1月22日在他的家乡享年50岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: León
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Ferdinand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Spanish name, originally Ferdinando (now Hernán), which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, derived from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. This was a traditional name in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It appeared in Britain in the 16th century, having been introduced by Roman Catholic supporters of Queen Mary I , who married Philip II of Spain in 1554 .

variants.name: Fernand
meanings: From a Spanish name, originally Ferdinando (now Hernán), which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, derived from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. This was a traditional name in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It appeared in Britain in the 16th century, having been introduced by Roman Catholic supporters of Queen Mary I , who married Philip II of Spain in 1554 .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Ferdinand II De León was born on 15 August 1137, in Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spain, his father, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, was 32 and his mother, Berenguela de Barcelona, was 21. He married Urraca Of Portugal in June 1165. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 22 January 1188, in his hometown, at the age of 50, and was buried in León, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ferdinand II De León est né le 15 août 1137, à Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Berenguela de Barcelona, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Urraca Of Portugal en juin 1165. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 22 janvier 1188, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à León, Castilla y León, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ferdinand II De León am 15. August 1137 in Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León 32 und seine Mutter Berenguela de Barcelona 21. Er hat im Juni 1165 Urraca Of Portugal geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 22. Januar 1188 im Alter von 50 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in León, Castilla y León, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ferdinand II De León è nato il il 15 agosto 1137 nel Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spagna, il suo padre, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Berenguela de Barcelona, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Urraca Of Portugal nel giugno 1165. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 22 gennaio 1188, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a León, Castilla y León, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Ferdinand II De Leónが1137年8月15日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンザモーアベナベントで生まれた時，父Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y Leónは32歳，母Berenguela de Barcelonaは21歳であった。1165年6月にUrraca Of Portugalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1188年1月22日に故郷で50歳で死去し，スペインカスティラ イ レoンレoンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ferdinand II De León(이)가 1137년 8월 15일 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o 자모라 베나븐트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Berenguela de Barcelona(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1165년 6월 Urraca Of Portugal(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1188년 1월 22일 그의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 레o에 묻혔다.

pt.15 August 1137–22 January 1188: 15 de agosto de 1137–22 de janeiro de 1188
pt.Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spai: Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Espanha
pt.Palencia, Provincia de Palencia, Castill: Palencia, Palencia, Castilla y León, Espanha
pt.Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spai: Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Espanha
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ferdinand II De León nasceu em 15 de agosto de 1137, em Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Berenguela de Barcelona, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Urraca Of Portugal em junho de 1165. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 22 de janeiro de 1188, em sua cidade natal, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em León, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spai: бенавент, замора, кастилла и леoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ferdinand II De León родился 15 августа 1137 г. в местности бенавент, замора, кастилла и леoн, Испания, его отцу по имени Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Berenguela de Barcelona, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Urraca Of Portugal июнь 1165 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 22 января 1188 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности леoн, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, Spai: Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, España
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ferdinand II De León nació el 15 de agosto de 1137, en Benavente, Zamora, Castilla y León, España, su papá, Alfonso Raimundez VII Rey de Castilla y León, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Berenguela de Barcelona, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Urraca Of Portugal en junio de 1165. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 22 de enero de 1188, en su pueblo, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en León, Castilla y León, España.
